videos.title: Is ontology replacing the semantic model?
videos.description:
Everyone is asking the same question:
"Is Microsoft Fabric Ontology replacing semantic models?"

At first glance, it seems like it might. Both sit above your data, provide business definitions, and help users understand information without digging through raw tables.

But that's where the similarity ends.

In this video, Marthe explains why Fabric Ontology is not simply a better semantic model and why the distinction matters—especially as AI agents become a core part of the analytics experience.

videos.title: The ONLY thing AI will NEVER replace #Career #FutureOfWork #ArtificialIntelligence
videos.description:
AI keeps making it easier to build — and harder to stand out. When the platforms and playbooks reset every few months, the people who win aren't the fastest builders. They're the ones with trust and relationships that compound.

That's the part nobody can automate. Here's the full breakdown — why the bottleneck moved and what's actually scarce now 👉 https://natesnewsletter.substack.com/p/8-habits-worth-unlearning-in-the

#AI #Career #FutureOfWork #Relationships #ArtificialIntelligence

videos.title: I Stopped Prompting AI One Task At A Time. This Works Better.
videos.description:
Full post w/ Questionnaire:
https://natesnewsletter.substack.com/p/ai-loop-managers?r=1z4sm5&utm_campaign=post&utm_medium=web&showWelcomeOnShare=true

AI agents get useful when you stop prompting one task at a time and start building loops. A loop is a recurring job that remembers, notices what changed, and stops where your judgment matters.

videos.title: 5 Ways Claude Tag Could Change How You Use AI
videos.description:
Claude Tag embeds persistent, proactive AI coworkers into Slack channels to access team context, manage long‑horizon tasks, and automate code and incident response. Regulatory flashpoints include the Anthropic Fable access ban, a lawsuit seeking model reinstatement, and government pressure on model testing. Additional discussions cover US scrutiny of Chinese robotics, new goal‑directed coding agents from xAI and Groq, and ByteDance's C‑Dance 2.5 advances in video generation.

The AI Daily Brief
